instruction: liste les 5 objets les plus consultes au cours des 30 derniers jours
sql_bad: SELECT OBJECT_NAME, COUNT(*) AS NB FROM ORACLE_AUDIT_TRAIL WHERE EVENT_TIMESTAMP>=SYSDATE-30 ORDER BY NB DESC FETCH FIRST 5 ROWS ONLY;
sql_good: SELECT OBJECT_NAME, COUNT(*) AS NB FROM ORACLE_AUDIT_TRAIL WHERE ACTION_NAME='SELECT' AND OBJECT_NAME IS NOT NULL AND EVENT_TIMESTAMP>=SYSDATE-30 GROUP BY OBJECT_NAME ORDER BY NB DESC FETCH FIRST 5 ROWS ONLY;

instruction: quels utilisateurs se sont connectes entre 22h et 6h du matin ?
sql_bad: SELECT DBUSERNAME, EVENT_TIMESTAMP FROM ORACLE_AUDIT_TRAIL WHERE ACTION_NAME='LOGON' AND TO_NUMBER(TO_CHAR(EVENT_TIMESTAMP,'HH24'))<6 ORDER BY EVENT_TIMESTAMP DESC;
sql_good: SELECT DBUSERNAME, EVENT_TIMESTAMP FROM ORACLE_AUDIT_TRAIL WHERE ACTION_NAME='LOGON' AND (TO_NUMBER(TO_CHAR(EVENT_TIMESTAMP,'HH24'))>=22 OR TO_NUMBER(TO_CHAR(EVENT_TIMESTAMP,'HH24'))<6) ORDER BY EVENT_TIMESTAMP DESC;

pattern: 2 semaines mal interpretees en filtre horaire
instruction: y a t il un utilisateur qui s est connecte durant les deux dernieres semaines ?
sql_bad: SELECT DBUSERNAME, EVENT_TIMESTAMP FROM ORACLE_AUDIT_TRAIL WHERE ACTION_NAME='LOGON' AND TO_NUMBER(TO_CHAR(EVENT_TIMESTAMP,'HH24'))>=22 AND TRUNC(EVENT_TIMESTAMP)>=TRUNC(SYSDATE-7) ORDER BY EVENT_TIMESTAMP DESC;
sql_good: SELECT DBUSERNAME, EVENT_TIMESTAMP FROM ORACLE_AUDIT_TRAIL WHERE ACTION_NAME='LOGON' AND TRUNC(EVENT_TIMESTAMP)>=TRUNC(SYSDATE-14) ORDER BY EVENT_TIMESTAMP DESC;

instruction: qu est ce qui s est passe dans la base hier ?
sql_bad: SELECT DBUSERNAME, ACTION_NAME, EVENT_TIMESTAMP FROM ORACLE_AUDIT_TRAIL WHERE TRUNC(EVENT_TIMESTAMP)=TRUNC(SYSDATE-1) ORDER BY EVENT_TIMESTAMP DESC;
sql_good: SELECT DBUSERNAME, ACTION_NAME, OBJECT_NAME, EVENT_TIMESTAMP FROM ORACLE_AUDIT_TRAIL WHERE TRUNC(EVENT_TIMESTAMP)=TRUNC(SYSDATE-1) ORDER BY EVENT_TIMESTAMP DESC;

instruction: qui a fait le dernier create dans la base ?
sql_bad: SELECT DBUSERNAME, OBJECT_NAME, EVENT_TIMESTAMP FROM ORACLE_AUDIT_TRAIL WHERE ACTION_NAME='CREATE TABLE' ORDER BY EVENT_TIMESTAMP DESC FETCH FIRST 1 ROW ONLY;
sql_good: SELECT DBUSERNAME, ACTION_NAME, OBJECT_NAME, EVENT_TIMESTAMP FROM ORACLE_AUDIT_TRAIL WHERE ACTION_NAME IN ('CREATE TABLE','CREATE USER') ORDER BY EVENT_TIMESTAMP DESC FETCH FIRST 1 ROW ONLY;
